info.name: Macro Workspace Storage API
info.description:
Test collection for the unified Workspace Storage API (contract: docs/api/workspace-storage-api.openapi.yaml).

**Setup**
1. Start the phase-1 reference server: `npm run start:market-data-server` (serves the API at http://localhost:3000/workspace/v1).
2. Import this collection. Variables: `baseUrl` (point at DEV/UAT/PROD or the local reference), `userId` (sent as `X-User-Id` on every user-scoped request — the interim auth scheme; phase 2 replaces it with a real identity token).

**Conventions under test**
- PUT is an upsert: 201 when created, 200 when replaced; body id must equal the path id (400 otherwise).
- GET of a missing single resource → 404 problem+json (the OpenFin client maps this to `undefined`).
- DELETE of a missing resource → 404 (the client treats it as success — idempotent).
- Single-resource GETs and PUT responses carry an `ETag`; optional `If-Match` on PUT/DELETE → 412 on mismatch.
- Errors are RFC 9457 `application/problem+json`.
- `/health`, `/config/*`, `/dock-apps` and `/store-apps` are NOT user-scoped (`X-User-Id` is ignored — LOB dock and store apps are shared across all users).

Run the whole collection top-to-bottom (Collection Runner or `npx newman run …`) — later requests reuse ETags captured by earlier ones, and every run creates what it deletes.

request.description: Liveness — not user-scoped, no X-User-Id needed.

exec: pm.test('201 created (or 200 replaced on re-run)', () => pm.expect([200, 201]).to.include(pm.response.code));

request.description: Upsert by path id. The snapshot is opaque JSON to the server — the OpenFin client owns its schema.

exec: pm.test('200 + array containing the test workspace', () => {

request.description: The OpenFin client maps this 404 to `undefined`. NOTE: a 404 on PUT would mean a misrouted baseUrl and the client THROWS there instead.
Uses the ETag captured by 'GET single workspace'. Run the folder in order.

exec: pm.test('404 (client treats as success — idempotent)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(404));

request.description: The user's rearranged dock (DockProviderConfigWithIdentity) — `id` must match the path.

exec: pm.test('200 + appIds array (empty when never saved)', () => {

request.description: FULL-list replace — the OpenFin client gates this on a successful prior load so a never-hydrated cache can't wipe stored favorites.
Well-known keys used by the platform: theme-preset, view-titles, last-saved-workspace, pages-migrated. Any JSON value goes in the { value } wrapper.

Platform config served per environment. Valid names: settings, apps, dock-config, storefront-config, snap-config, entitlements. In REST storage mode the OpenFin client loads apps/dock-config/storefront-config/snap-config from here (settings + entitlements stay static — bootstrap tier).

exec: pm.test('404 for unknown config name', () => pm.response.to.have.status(404));
pm.test('501 Not Implemented in the reference server', () => pm.response.to.have.status(501));

request.description: Config writes are an admin operation for the phase-2 service; the reference implementation returns 501.

Custom dock apps published by lines of business — SHARED across users (like /config/*), so no X-User-Id. The OpenFin client (getLobDockApps) renders them into the dock at init: `icon` → a dock button, `dropdown` → a dock folder. Always 200 with an array, sorted by sortOrder ascending (undefined last, stable).

exec: pm.test('201 created (or 200 replaced on re-run)', () => pm.expect([200, 201]).to.include(pm.response.code));

request.description: Upsert by path id (body.id must match). `type: icon` requires a non-empty `url`; `iconUrl` is always required. This is the LOB publish path — Postman/curl/LOB tooling writes, the platform only reads.

`type: dropdown` requires a non-empty `children` array; each child needs id/label/url (child iconUrl is optional — falls back to the parent's). sortOrder 1 sorts this BEFORE lob-rates-monitor (sortOrder 2).
List order is the dock render order: sortOrder ascending, entries without sortOrder last, ties stable.

exec: pm.test('400 problem+json for icon app missing url', () => {

request.description: Validation: `type: icon` without a `url` must be rejected with a helpful problem+json detail — a dock button with nothing to launch would render dead.

exec: pm.test('400 problem+json for dropdown with empty children', () => {

request.description: Validation: `type: dropdown` requires a NON-EMPTY `children` array — an empty dock folder is rejected.
Uses the ETag captured by 'GET single lob-rates-monitor'. Run the folder in order.
No If-Match — unconditional delete. Cleanup so every run creates what it deletes.

Full storefront apps published by lines of business — SHARED across users (like /config/* and /dock-apps), so no X-User-Id. The OpenFin client (getLobStoreApps) merges them into the app registry at load: they appear in the Storefront, Home search, and launch per their manifestType. Always 200 with an array, sorted by sortOrder ascending (undefined last, stable).

exec: pm.test('201 created (or 200 replaced on re-run)', () => pm.expect([200, 201]).to.include(pm.response.code));

request.description: Upsert by path appId (body.appId must match). A `view` app: `manifest` points at a view manifest URL. Full details-page payload — description, images (screenshots), publisher and support/contact emails. This is the LOB publish path — Postman/curl/LOB tooling writes, the platform only reads.
`manifestType: manifest` — launches another platform from its platform manifest. Only the required fields plus lob/sortOrder. sortOrder 1 sorts this BEFORE lob-rates-analytics (sortOrder 2).
List order is the storefront render order: sortOrder ascending, entries without sortOrder last, ties stable.

exec: pm.test('400 problem+json for empty icons array', () => {

request.description: Validation: `icons` must be a NON-EMPTY array of {src} — a store card without an icon is rejected with a helpful problem+json detail.
Validation: `manifestType` is an enum — only 'view' or 'manifest' are accepted; 'snapshot' is rejected.
Uses the ETag captured by 'GET single lob-rates-analytics'. Run the folder in order.
No If-Match — unconditional delete. Cleanup so every run creates what it deletes.

item.name: 2. List as {{altUserId}} → must NOT see it

request.description: Same route, different X-User-Id — the other user's data must be invisible.
Optimistic concurrency: the phase-2 service surfaces a Mongo version field as the ETag; a stale If-Match must 412 rather than last-write-wins.
